LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT OLIVER PARKER AND MICROSOFT CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND FOR REASONS STATED ON THE RECORD. AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH ECONOMIC RELATIONSHIP: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE AS TO MICROSOFT: SUSTAINED BECAUSE THE CLAIM AS STATED DOES NOT STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION AND IS PREEMPTED BY WORKERS COMPENSATION. AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS: SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD, IF PLAINTIFF CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, CONDUCT BY DEFENDANTS WHICH IS EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS, BEYOND POSSIBLE BOUNDS OF HUMAN DECENCY. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AS TO BOTH MICROSOFT AND PARKER: SUSTAINED FOR THE SAME REASON AS THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THESE DEFENDANTS. PLAINTIFF TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN TEN DAYS AFTER NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077.

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS OLIVER PARKER AND MICROSOFT CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO DEFENDANTS FIRST REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO OPPOSE THE MOTION TO COMPEL AND THE FACTS REVEAL THAT PLAINTIFF FAILED TO RESPOND SEPARATELY TO EACH DOCUMENT REQUEST PURSUANT TO CCP 2031.210(A). PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO AMEND HIS RESPONSES TO PROVIDE SEPARATE RESPONSES TO EACH OF THE 49 DOCUMENT REQUESTS WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THE HEARING AND TO PRODUCE THE DOCUMENTS AT THAT TIME AS WELL. THE RESPONSE MUST EITHER AFFIRM HE WILL COMPLY WITH THE REQUEST OR AFFIRM A DILIGENT SEARCH AND REASONABLY INQUIRY WAS MADE AND SPECIFY WHETHER THE INABILITY TO COMPLY IS BECAUSE A DOCUMENT NEVER EXISTED, WAS LOST OR MISPLACED, OR HAS NEVER BEEN OR IS NO LONGER IN PLAINTIFF'S LEGAL POSSESSION, CUSTODY OR CONTROL. SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,500 IS GRANTED WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THE ORDER. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS). JUDGE PRO TEM: ARLENE BARTON, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED.=302/JPT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continue Trial
Off Calendar Oct-07-2014 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Order;
Off Calendar Jun-26-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kristen Jacoby, Esq., 415.773.5847, for defendants Microsoft Corporation and Oliver Parker; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MARK BIRKHEAD'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Discovery Order is denied. Plaintiff did not file or serve opposition to Defendants' Motion to Compel Further Response to Defendants' First Request for Production of Documents that was heard on April 3, 2014. Plaintiff received the tentative ruling prior to the hearing and affirmatively communicated to both the Court and opposing counsel that he would not appear or contest the tentative ruling. "The tentative ruling will become the ruling of the court if the court has not directed oral argument by its tentative ruling and notice of intent to appear has not been given." (CRC Rule 3.1308(a)(1).) Further, Plaintiff did not appear at the hearing and is deemed to have stipulated to the attorney serving as a temporary judge. (CRC 2.816(d)(1).) Accordingly, Plaintiff has not made a sufficient showing to obtain relief pursuant to CCP 473 and any relief pursuant to CCP 1008 has long expired. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

RULING:
The tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANTS OLIVER PARKER and MICROSOFT CORPORATION's Motion To Compel Further Response To Defendants First Request For Production Of Documents is Granted. Plaintiff failed to Oppose the motion to compel and the facts reveal that Plaintiff failed to respond separately to each document request pursuant to CCP 2031.210(a). CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-12-2013 Continued To Aug-07-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARK BIRKHEAD, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
CHRISTIAN BROWN, ESQ., 415.773.5816, FOR DEFENDANTS OLIVER PARKER AND MICROSOFT CORPORATION.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS OLIVER PARKER, MICROSOFT CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS overruled as to the first, second, fourth and fifth causes of action. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the third cause of action against Microsoft Corporation. Sustained, without leave to amend, as to second cause of action against Mr. Parker. For the first cause of action, threatening to destroy a career, as alleged, is not within routine personnel management activity. (Yurick v. Sup.Ct. (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 1116, 1128.) The claim is not clearly preempted by workers compensation exclusivity. For the second cause of action, Plaintiff has pled a number of acts and statutory bases that implicate fundamental public policy concerns against Microsoft. (Foley v. Interactive Data Corp. (1988) 47 Cal.3d 654, 669.) A Tameny tort claim cannot be maintained against individual supervisory employees like Mr. Parker. (Reno v. Baird (1998) 18 Cal.4th 640, 663.) For the third cause of action, Plaintiff must allege "the names of the persons who made the allegedly fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written." (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 157.) For the fourth cause of action, the defamation claim is not clearly barred by the Statute of Limitations; Plaintiff alleges that the defamation occurred on June 2, 2011 and various other times. (FAC, 53.) The alleged defamatory statement that Plaintiff "is taking money under the table (bribes) from Cisco Systems" is neither vague nor imprecise. (Ibid.) For the fifth cause of action, although conspiracy is not a cause of action, "it is a legal doctrine that imposes liability on persons who, although not actually committing a tort themselves, share with the immediate tortfeasors a common plan or design in its perpetration . . . ." (Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 510-511.) Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege a conspiracy. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend as to the third cause of action only. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: L. Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Off Calendar Jan-16-2013 Continued To Apr-03-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kristen M. Jacoby, Esq. of Orrick Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP, Tel. 415.773.5847, for defendant and moving party;
Mark Birkhead, Tel. 619.206.4084, plaintiff and opposing party appearing on his behalf.

RULING: The matter was argued. After hearing, the court ruled as follows: DEFENDANT OLIVER PARKER and MICROSOFT CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend for reasons stated on the record. As to the 1st cause of action for intentional interference with economic relationship: sustained without leave to amend. As to the 2nd cause of action for negligence as to Microsoft: sustained because the claim as stated does not state a cause of action and is preempted by workers compensation. As to the 3rd cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress: sustained with leave to amend to plead, if plaintiff can do so in good faith, conduct by defendants which is extreme and outrageous, beyond possible bounds of human decency. As to the 4th cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress as to both Microsoft and Parker: sustained for the same reason as the negligence cause of action. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead, if he can do so in good faith, a cause of action against these defendants. Plaintiff to file amended complaint within ten days after notice of entry of order. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077.
